Introduction

Welcome to PDAX API for Institutions Documentation.

The PDAX API is organized around the following workflows:
- Conversion of fiat to crypto and crypto to fiat.
- Withdrawals for Fiat or Crypto.
- Deposits for Fiat and Crypto.
- Viewing of balances and withdrawals.

Our API has predictable resource-oriented URLs, accepts form-encoded request bodies, returns JSON-encoded responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes, authentication, and verbs.

Getting Started

Use the following environments for your intended purpose. For Stage environment credentials, kindly contact your relationship manager to request for access.

Environment Base URL
Production https://services.pdax.ph/api/pdax-api
Stage https://stage.services.sandbox.pdax.ph/api/pdax-api

Authentication

Once you obtain your credentials, log in to your account and expect an access token and id token response which shall be used to authorize your requests.

POST Login

HTTP request

POST /pdax-institution/v1/login

Sample Request:

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/login' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
  "username": "user@domain.com",
  "password": "P@ssw0rd"
}'

Without MFA Setup
Sample Response (200):

{
    "email": "example@gmail.com",
    "username": "96a8c8b5-8fee-40c9-9242-a5cd172e9a96",
    "groups": [
        "exchange_user"
    ],
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "preferred_mfa": "SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA",
    "expiry": 600,
    "access_token": "eyJraWQiOiJ4UjJ2U1A2",
    "id_token": "eyJraWQiOiJsQzlPbldQV2ZX",
    "refresh_token": "eyJjdHkiOiJKV1QiLCJ",
}

With MFA Setup
Sample Response (200):

{
  "code": "AuthChallengeRequired",
  "message": "An authentication challenge is configured for this account.",
  "challenge_name": "SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA",
  "session": "{{SESSION_TOKEN}}"
}

Schema of Body Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
username string yes Email address of PDAX account
password string yes Password of PDAX account

Schema of Response Payload (Without MFA Setup)

Key Type Description
email string Email address of PDAX account
username string ID number of PDAX account
groups array List of groups user belongs to
token_type string Type of authentication token (e.g., "Bearer")
preferred_mfa string MFA preference status (e.g., "NOT_SET", "SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA")
expiry number Token validity period in seconds
access_token string JWT token for API authorization
id_token string JWT token for user identity
refresh_token string Token to refresh authentication without reauthentication

Schema of Response Payload (With MFA Setup)

Key Type Description
code string Response code from authentication service
message string Descriptive message about the MFA challenge
challenge_name string Type of challenge required (e.g., "SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA")
session string Session token for completing the MFA challenge

POST OTP

This endpoint is required if you enable MFA on your account

HTTP request

POST /pdax-institution/v1/login/otp

Sample Request:

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/login/otp' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
  "session":"{{SESSION_TOKEN}}",
  "username": "example@gmail.com",
  "otp": "676788"
}'

Sample Response (200):

{
    "email": "example@gmail.com",
    "username": "96a8c8b5-8fee-40c9-9242-a5cd172e9a96",
    "groups": [
        "exchange_user"
    ],
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "preferred_mfa": "SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA",
    "expiry": 600,
    "access_token": "eyJraWQiOiJ4UjJ2U1A2",
    "id_token": "eyJraWQiOiJsQzlPbldQV2ZX",
    "refresh_token": "eyJjdHkiOiJKV1QiLCJ",
}

Schema of Body Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
session string yes MFA session token
username string yes Email address of PDAX account
otp string yes One-time password code

Schema of Response Payload

Key Type Description

username string ID number of PDAX account
groups array List of groups user belongs to
token_type string Type of authentication token (e.g., "Bearer")
preferred_mfa string MFA preference status (e.g., "SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA")
expiry number Token validity period in seconds
access_token string JWT token for API authorization
id_token string JWT token for user identity
refresh_token string Token to refresh authentication without reauthentication

PUT Refresh Token

  • You may use the refresh token to keep your current log in session. Use the refresh token given in the response
    from the endpoint POST /pdax-institution/v1/login
  • Input the refreshToken in the request body of the refresh token API
  • use the access_token and id_token when authenticating subsequent API calls

HTTP request

PUT /pdax-institution/v1/refresh-token

Sample Request:

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/refresh-token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
  "username": "48e0fe9e-0724-4b80-9eb7-c61b53cd7346",
  "refreshToken": "eyJraWQiOiJwTXRndmx1UGQ1ZG5YK2U1MXNLQUFSSUpiTW00TnA5SzFUdlZDK0xLVmY4PSIsImFsZyI6IlJTMjU2In0"
}'

Sample Response (200):

{
    "email": "test@pdax.ph",
    "username": "48e0fe9e-0724-4b80-9eb7-c61b53cd7346",
    "groups": [
        "insti_user"
    ],
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "preferred_mfa": "NOT_SET",
    "expiry": 600,
    "access_token": "eyJraWQiOiJwTXRndmx1UGQ1ZG5YK2U1MXNLQUFSSUpiTW00TnA5SzFUdlZDK0xLVmY4PSIsImFsZyI6IlJTMjU2In0.",
    "id_token": "eyJraWQiOiJ6dU5uUkVqVmNpUTZ5ektqZEhUOVZoZGZtUzdSSkpzQjQ4ZUZ3S04wdUxZPSIsImFsZyI6IlJTMjU2In0",

Trade

GET Indicative Quote

Get a price quotation for the selected cryptopair.

HTTP request

GET /pdax-institution/v1/trade/price

Sample Request:

curl --location --request GET 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/trade/price?quote_currency=USDC&base_currency=PHP&side=sell&base_quantity=100'\
--header 'access_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--header 'id_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'

Sample Response (200):

{
  "data": {
    "quote_currency": "USDC",
    "base_currency": "PHP",
    "side": "sell",
    "base_quantity": "100",
    "price": 55,
    "total_amount": 5500,
  },
  "status": "success"
}

Schema of Query Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
quote_currency string yes The crypto asset you want to buy or sell .

Supported trading pairs can be found here
base_currency string yes PHP asset
side string yes Counterparty action -- buy or sell
base_quantity string yes Quantity to buy or sell.

Note:
  • Quantity step rules apply. For more information, see here

Schema of Response Payload

Key Type Description
quote_currency string The crypto asset you want to buy or sell .

Supported trading pairs can be found here
base_currency string PHP asset
side string Counterparty action -- buy or sell
base_quantity string Quantity of the asset you are buying or selling
price decimal Quoted unit price
total_amount decimal Total amount

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/trade/quote'\
--header 'access_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--header 'id_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--data-raw '{
  "quote_currency": "USDC",
  "base_currency": "PHP",
  "side": "sell",
  "base_quantity": "100"
}'

{

    "quote_id": "0188b945-cbfa-7b8b-9fea-bfcc2ac25c8c",

Supported trading pairs can be found here

POST Order

Place a market order using the firm quote reference. To check if the order has been completed, use the Get Order Status endpoint.

HTTP request

POST /pdax-institution/v1/trade

Sample Request:

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/trade'\
--header 'access_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--header 'id_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--data-raw '{
  "quote_id": "a7547037-a3eb-4380-b45d-4d92b801ab67",
  "side": "sell",
  "idempotency_id": "0188b945-1111-1111-9fea-bfcc2ac25c8c"
}'

Sample Response (200):

{
  "data": {
    "order_id": 122121,
    "status": "successful",
    "quote_currency": "USDC",
    "base_currency": "PHP",
    "side": "sell",
    "base_quantity": 100,
    "price": 55,
    "total_amount": 5500,
  },
   "status": "success"
}

Schema of Body Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
quote_id string yes Firm quote reference ID
side string yes Describes the side of the trade; this is dependent on the value of the side from Get Firm Quote
idempotency_id string yes Id of the request made. You may generate an idempotency id by using uuid ver 4 format

Sample here: https://www.uuidgenerator.net/version4

Schema of Response Payload

Key Type Description
order_id number Id of the order
status string Possible Values : successful, failed
quote_currency string If side is 'Buy'
  • Quote currency is the asset you want to receive
If side is 'Sell'
  • Quote currency is the asset you want to sell
base_currency string If side is 'Buy'
  • Base currency is the asset you want to sell
If side is 'Sell'
  • Base currency is the asset you want to receive
side string Counterparty side
base_quantity decimal Order quantity as per firm quote
price decimal Quoted unit price
total_amount decimal Total amount

GET Order Status

Use this endpoint to verify the status of your order.

HTTP request

GET /pdax-institution/v1/orders/{order_id}

Sample Request:

curl --location --request GET 'https://API_DOMAIN/v1/orders/122121'\
--header 'access_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--header 'id_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\

Sample Response (200):

{
  "data": {
    "order_id": "122121",
    "status": "SUCCESSFUL",
    "quote_currency": "USDC",
    "base_currency": "PHP",
    "side": "sell",
    "base_quantity": 100,
    "price": 55,
    "total_amount": 5500,
  },
  "status": "success"
}

Schema of Path Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
order_id string yes Order ID returned from Post Order endpoint

Schema of Response Payload

Key Type Description
order_id number Order id
status string Possible Values : SUCCESSFUL, FAILED
quote_currency string If side is 'Buy'
  • Quote currency is the asset you want to receive
If side is 'Sell'
  • Quote currency is the asset you want to sell
base_currency string If side is 'Buy'
  • Base currency is the asset you want to sell
If side is 'Sell'
  • Base currency is the asset you want to receive
side string Counterparty side
base_quantity decimal Order quantity as per firm quote
price decimal Quoted unit price
total_amount decimal Indicative total price inclusive of fees

Funding

Get Wallet (for Crypto Deposits)

Deposit into your crypto wallet using the address returned by this endpoint.

HTTP Request

GET /pdax-institution/v1/crypto/deposit

Sample Request:

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/crypto/deposit?currency=USDCXLM'\
--header 'access_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--header 'id_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\

Sample Response (200):

{
  "data": {
    "currency": "USDCXLM",
    "address": "GA54SPC34JL3I57ENALTO2V26XOFFG4VGQLFQXDGF6KJ5TJY7ODY56ST",
    "tag": "123123123"
  }
}

Schema of Query Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
currency string yes Currency symbol including network of crypto token

e.g USDCXLM

Schema of Response Payload

Returns a list of wallet addresses with the following keys:

Key Type Description
currency string Currency symbol
address string Wallet address
tag string Address tag or memo

Withdrawals

POST Fiat Out

Withdraw Fiat from your account to a beneficiary

HTTP request

POST /pdax-institution/v1/fiat/withdraw

Sample Request:

curl --location --request POST 'https://API_DOMAIN/pdax-institution/v1/fiat/withdraw'\
--header 'access_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--header 'id_token: eyJraWQiOiJDZEVOVnNTdWJRS09saG9lQTVvOT...'\
--data-raw --data '{
    "identifier": "ABC123",

POST Crypto Out

Withdraw Crypto from your account to a beneficiary

HTTP request

Withdraw crypto from your account to a beneficiary wallet or exchange

POST /pdax-institution/v1/crypto/withdraw

Note:
  • We need to collect additional data for transactions equivalent to PHP 50,000 or more. This ensures compliance with the Travel Rule regulation by BSP.

children.value: Welcome to PDAX API for Institutions Documentation.
The PDAX API is organized around the following workflows:
- Conversion of fiat to crypto and crypto to fiat.
Our API has predictable resource-oriented URLs, accepts form-encoded request bodies, returns JSON-encoded responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes, authentication, and verbs.
Use the following environments for your intended purpose. For Stage environment credentials, kindly contact your relationship manager to request for access.
Once you obtain your credentials, log in to your account and expect an
response which shall be used to authorize your requests.

List of groups user belongs to

Session token for completing the MFA challenge
This endpoint is required if you enable MFA on your account

Token to refresh authentication without reauthentication
You may use the refresh token to keep your current log in session. Use the refresh token given in the response
Input the refreshToken in the request body of the refresh token API
use the access_token and id_token when authenticating subsequent API calls
